sections.title: Safety Essentials
sections.summary: Planning a solo trip to New York City for the first time? NYC is generally a strong solo-travel destination because the busiest visitor areas have steady foot traffic, frequent transit, and plenty of places to step inside if you need a reset.
bullets: Stay aware on the subway, avoid empty cars late at night, and move cars at the next stop if something feels off.
Use well-lit streets and stick to main avenues after dark.
Keep your bag in front of you in crowded areas.
Trust your instincts, step into a hotel lobby or busy shop if needed, and call 911 for emergencies or 311 for non-emergency city help.
